source.arrivalCorridor: FAA-published Oshkosh VFR Arrival Procedures (Ripon Transition), via RST Engineering
source.facts: EAA AirVenture Oshkosh 2025 Facts and Figures (eaa.org), FAA AirVenture domestic notice

runways.note: Longest runway on the field — primary jet/warbird runway, tower 126.6
Tower 118.5 — during AirVenture, the busiest control tower frequency in the world
Shortest runway — used for ultralights and light homebuilts during AirVenture

arrivalCorridor.name: The Fisk Arrival (Ripon Transition)
arrivalCorridor.description: The world-famous VFR arrival used by thousands of pilots flying into AirVenture. Aircraft funnel into a single-file line over a railroad track at 90 knots and 1,800 ft, exactly ½ mile apart, with radios silent — a controller on the ground at Fisk aims colored flags and gives instructions pilots acknowledge only by rocking their wings.

checkpoints.name: Ripon
checkpoints.note: Entry point — small-town water tower pilots fly over at 1,800 ft and 90 kt to join the line

checkpoints.name: Pickett
checkpoints.note: Midpoint of the railroad-track single-file line

checkpoints.name: Fisk
checkpoints.note: The hold-short point — a controller on the ground assigns each aircraft a runway and a colored dot using only flags and light signals, no radio

facts.label: Busiest tower on Earth
facts.detail: For this one week, KOSH's runway 9/27 tower frequency becomes the busiest air traffic control tower in the world.

facts.label: 2025 attendance
facts.detail: Aggregate attendance across the 2025 show — EAA's official figure.

facts.label: 2025 aircraft operations
facts.detail: Takeoffs and landings at Wittman Field alone over the show, ~108 per hour while the field was open.

facts.label: Showplanes (2025)
facts.detail: 995 vintage, 910 homebuilt, 361 warbirds, 101 ultralights, 75 aerobatic, 74 seaplanes, 18 rotorcraft, 9 balloons.

facts.label: Land on the dot
facts.detail: Controllers assign arriving aircraft to land on a colored dot painted on the runway — white, green, or orange — with zero radio contact.
